headline: Educators are converging on the production execution layer for AI agents, from interruptible voice pipelines to local agentic coding models.
body: The recommended channels lead on agent production readiness: TEN Framework [0] tackles natural voice conversation through graph-based extensions and real-time pipelines covering VAD, STT, TTS, and interruption handling, while Isadora Martin-Dye [4] argues for a four-layer brand voice architecture ending in a deterministic post-generation veto rather than single-pass tone instructions. Tooling and memory dominate the rest: Chase AI [3] surveys 17 Claude plugins spanning Playwright, GitHub CLI, Firecrawl, and code-generation skills, and Iusztin and Bouchard [12] convert 10,000+ Obsidian notes into a three-layer memory system for deep research agents. On models and training, AICodeKing [13] covers Ornith, an MIT-licensed 9B-397B family from DeepReinforce for local agentic coding, and Dwarkesh Patel [8] maps the next training paradigm around RLVR, test-time compute, and dreaming. Alex Kantrowitz [7] frames the macro debate at the Big Technology AI Summit around a hypothetical federal recall of Anthropic models as a potential AI pause.

cited_items.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
